thesis_title: Authentic Human Behavioral Data as AI Training Signal: An Information-Theoretic Framework
thesis_statement: Training data authenticity, as captured by the proposed L_auth framework, systematically influences model quality — particularly on socially grounded tasks. This claim is supported by two independent argument lines: (1) pretraining-stage collapse theory and web pollution risk, and (2) fine-tuning-stage evidence that data provenance and social behavioral diversity affect social reasoning performance.

line_1.label: Pretraining layer (risk argument)
line_1.scope: Establishes that data authenticity matters strategically at the pretraining stage. This line builds a risk case, NOT a proven degradation claim.

line_2.label: Fine-tuning layer (experimental argument)
line_2.scope: Establishes that data provenance and social diversity affect fine-tuning outcomes on social reasoning tasks. This line has its own literature base independent of collapse theory.

bridge.label: L_auth as stage-agnostic framework
bridge.scope: L_auth unifies the two lines at a higher level of abstraction: 'data authenticity composition affects training outcomes'. It does NOT claim that the mechanisms are the same across stages.

proposal.label: CampusGo design proposal
proposal.scope: Motivated by both lines. Supported only as a design direction, NOT as a validated solution.

beats.title: Model Collapse and Contamination Risk (Pretraining Layer)
beats.claim: Recursive training on synthetic data creates a conditional collapse risk. Reactive detection/filtering is fragile. But collapse is NOT universal — mixed-data regimes, correction mechanisms, and curated synthetic data can mitigate it.
must_cite_counterevidence: pi^2/6 pathway: mixed real+synthetic avoids collapse
Self-correcting loops: correction functions stabilize self-consuming training
Curated synthetic data: can optimize preference objectives under strong curation
honesty_constraints: Do NOT claim all synthetic data is harmful
Do NOT claim web-scale contamination is proven — only domain-specific evidence exists
Do NOT claim detection is impossible — only that it is fragile under adversarial conditions
search_queries: AI text detection watermarking adversarial evasion
AI generated content web pollution prevalence

beats.title: Partial Measurability of Web Drift (Pretraining Layer)
beats.claim: Web content quality drift is measurable through indirect proxies and longitudinal studies, but NOT yet causally linked to post-2022 AI contamination at web scale. Filtered web corpora (RefinedWeb, FineWeb) still produce strong models.
must_cite_counterevidence: RefinedWeb/FineWeb: filtered web data still trains strong models
Domain-specific contamination (Wikipedia) does not equal web-wide degradation
honesty_constraints: Do NOT claim web-wide pretraining degradation is demonstrated
Frame as 'rising risk' not 'demonstrated failure'
Explicitly state: no post-2022 web-scale contamination audit exists in the corpus
beats.transition_to_next: These two sections focused on pretraining-stage risks. Data authenticity also affects fine-tuning and alignment — the next section defines L_auth as a framework that spans both stages.
search_queries: web content quality temporal longitudinal measurement
lexical diversity entropy corpus measurement robustness

beats.title: L_auth Framework Definition (Bridge)
beats.claim: L_auth is a stage-agnostic descriptive framework with four dimensions: Provenance Ratio (D1), Lexical Diversity (D2), Entropy (D3), Social Behavioral Diversity (D4). D1 and D4 are design-level inputs; D2 and D3 are measurable emergent outcomes. L_auth is a synthesis of existing metric ingredients, NOT a validated law or a detection tool.

D1_provenance_ratio.definition: Proportion of data traceable to authentic human interaction

stage_operationalization.pretraining: fraction of non-AI-generated content in corpus
stage_operationalization.finetuning: fraction of instruction/dialogue data from real human interaction

D2_lexical_diversity.definition: MATTR (Moving Average TTR), NOT raw TTR (TTR is corpus-size sensitive)
literature_anchor: Entropy and TTR in gigaword corpora
D2_lexical_diversity.causal_position: emergent outcome of D1, not a direct optimization target

D3_entropy.definition: Word-level Shannon entropy of text distribution
D3_entropy.causal_position: emergent outcome of D1, not a direct optimization target

D4_social_behavioral_diversity.definition: Coverage of social interaction types based on Social IQa taxonomy
D4_social_behavioral_diversity.causal_position: design-level input, directly controllable by data collection strategy
D4_social_behavioral_diversity.note: This is the paper's unique contribution — connects L_auth to social reasoning evaluation

beats.weight_convention: No weights given. Equal-weight baseline used in experiment. Weight calibration is future work requiring 20+ condition factorial design.
honesty_constraints: Do NOT present L_auth as a validated law or discovery
Frame as 'grounded synthesis of existing metric ingredients'
Do NOT claim L_auth is a detection tool — it is a descriptive/predictive framework
Explicitly state that weight calibration is future work
search_queries: entropy type-token ratio lexical diversity corpus measurement
data provenance training corpus quality metric

beats.title: Fine-tuning Data Source Affects Social Reasoning (Fine-tuning Layer)
beats.claim: On social reasoning tasks, data provenance and curation quality matter. Curated human data can be disproportionately valuable (LIMA). But AI feedback can substitute on bounded tasks (RLAIF, AlpacaFarm). The defensible claim is narrower: human data appears especially valuable for socially grounded tasks, not that it universally dominates.
beats.independence_statement: This beat's argument is INDEPENDENT of Beat 1's collapse mechanism. Do NOT use pretraining collapse papers to support fine-tuning claims. Fine-tuning has its own literature base.
must_cite_counterevidence: RLAIF: AI feedback matches RLHF on summarization and dialogue
AlpacaFarm: simulated feedback 50x cheaper with high agreement
Zephyr 7B: AI feedback DPO beats LLaMA2-Chat-70B
Social-R1: targeted adversarial training improves social reasoning
Self-Instruct/WizardLM: synthetic instructions work for general instruction-following
beats.must_acknowledge_limitation: The corpus does NOT directly show AI-feedback or self-alignment failing on social-reasoning tasks. This domain restriction is a HYPOTHESIS, not an established result.
honesty_constraints: Do NOT claim human data is universally indispensable for alignment
Do NOT claim AI feedback fails on social tasks — corpus lacks direct evidence for this
Frame as 'especially valuable for socially grounded tasks' not 'always necessary'
search_queries: RLHF human feedback alignment preference data
RLAIF AI feedback vs human feedback
social reasoning Theory of Mind LLM benchmark
LIMA instruction tuning small dataset alignment

beats.title: Contrastive Fine-tuning Experiment (Fine-tuning Layer)
beats.claim: A pilot study comparing high/medium/low L_auth fine-tuning data on social reasoning benchmarks. Tests whether L_auth dimensions predict performance differences. Results are directional support, not complete validation.

experiment_design.analysis: Equal-weight L_auth dimension measurement + dimension separation observation + optional sensitivity analysis

honesty_constraints: Frame as 'pilot study' not 'validation'
Use 'initial evidence suggests' not 'demonstrates'
Acknowledge: 3B model may not amplify data quality differences sufficiently
Acknowledge: D1 and D4 co-vary in this design, cannot separate independent contributions
search_queries: LoRA QLoRA fine-tuning small language model evaluation
synthetic vs human data fine-tuning ablation

beats.title: CampusGo as Design Proposal
beats.claim: CampusGo is a campus social interaction platform designed to optimize L_auth D1 (provenance via .edu verification) and D4 (social behavioral diversity via four scenario types). It is a motivated design direction, NOT a validated solution.

design_logic.D1_optimization: .edu email verification ensures authentic student users
design_logic.D4_optimization: Four scenario types (rideshare, marketplace, activities, cross-cultural) create scene × behavior-type cross-coverage
design_logic.D2_D3_emergent: High D1 data naturally exhibits higher lexical diversity and entropy than LLM-generated data (supported by Curious Decline, ChatGPT is Average)
design_logic.cross_cultural_bonus: International campus environment adds code-switching and multicultural pragmatic diversity

honesty_constraints: Use proposal verbs: 'motivates' 'suggests' 'points toward' 'frames requirements for'
Do NOT use: 'demonstrates' 'proves' 'validates'
Acknowledge: G-category literature is from adjacent domains (citizen science, biomedicine, Indigenous data governance), not directly from AI training data collection
Acknowledge: platform effectiveness is untested
search_queries: campus smartphone sensing behavioral data collection
data provenance AI training pipeline transparency

citation_chain_requirements.method: Use Semantic Scholar API /paper/{id}/references endpoint to verify actual citation links between spine papers

narrative_verb_rules.no_citation_but_thematically_related: Use 'in a parallel line of inquiry' 'complementary evidence from' 'independently'

global_constraints.argument_line_separation: NEVER use pretraining collapse papers (Category A) to directly support fine-tuning claims (Sections 4-5). The two argument lines share L_auth as a bridge but have independent evidence bases.
overselling_watchlist: Do not claim human feedback is universally indispensable
Do not claim web-scale contamination is proven
Do not present L_auth as a validated law
Do not claim CampusGo is a validated solution
Do not equate pretraining collapse mechanism with fine-tuning data quality effects
global_constraints.weakest_dimension: honesty (scored 0.76 in iteration 1). Priority: reduce overselling risk.
global_constraints.stance_quantification: For each focus question with contradictions, report stance counts (side_a / side_b / ambiguous) so the corpus does not appear one-sided.
